outcome_contract.why: Staff must manually scan each room timeline to find today's check-ins and check-outs; there is no summary.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A "Today's Movements" section on the rooms-grid page lists arrivals (check-in date = today) and departures (check-out date = today) with room numbers and guest names, eliminating manual timeline scanning.

engineering_coverage.applicable: DS primitives (Cluster/Inline/Stack) only for layout; two-section layout: Arriving/Departing
Empty state ("No arrivals today" / "No departures today"), populated state; panel guarded by todayInWindow
N/A — reads in-memory data already loaded for the grid; no new data surface
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Internal tool, access-controlled by operator login

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — pure read-only display component; no mutations
Unit tests for TodayMovements (TC-01 to TC-04); RoomsGrid wiring test (TC-05)
No schema change; `TodayMovementEntry = { room, occupantId, firstName, lastName }` type defined in component file
O(rooms × rows × periods) — bounded; no async; useMemo guards re-computation
Direct commit deploy; rollback = revert commit; no migration or feature flag

stage_payload.summary: Add a compact "Today's Movements" panel to the rooms-grid page (`RoomsGrid.tsx`) showing which guests are arriving and departing today. All data is derived from the existing `getReservationDataForRoom` hook by iterating known rooms and filtering periods where `start === today` (arrivals) or `end === today` (departures). A new `TodayMovements` component accepts typed arrays as props and renders two lists using DS layout primitives. The panel appears only when today is within the visible grid window, matching the `OccupancyStrip` guard pattern.
goals: Render a "Today's Movements" panel on the rooms-grid page with arrivals and departures.
Follow the established DS-primitives pattern (Cluster, Inline, Stack).
Cover 4 unit TCs in a new test file + 1 RoomsGrid wiring assertion.
non_goals: Always-visible panel independent of grid window.
assumptions: `period.end === today` means guest departs today (exclusive-end convention).
`occupantId` is unique per booking occupant — safe as React render key.
selected_approach: New `TodayMovements.tsx` component accepting `arrivals: TodayMovementEntry[]` and `departures: TodayMovementEntry[]` props. Computation in `RoomsGrid` by iterating `knownRooms` + `getReservationDataForRoom`.
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Analysis clearly rejected Option B (new Firebase query) as unnecessary overhead. Option A is unambiguously correct.

task_briefs.title: Create TodayMovements component, wire into RoomsGrid, write tests

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change.
